2606.03685.title: A Close Look At World Model Recovery In Supervised Fine-Tuned LLM Planners
2606.03685.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) improves end-to-end classical planning in large language models (LLMs), but do these models also learn to represent and reason about the planning problems they are solving? Due to the relative complexity of classical planning problems and the challenge that end-to-end plan generation poses for LLMs, it has been difficult to explore this question. In our work, we devise and perform a series of interpretability experiments that holistically interrogate world model recovery by examining both internal representations and generative capabilities of fine-tuned LLMs. We find that: a) Supervised fine-tuning on valid action sequences enables LLMs to linearly encode action validity and some state predicates. b) Models that struggle to use output probabilities for classifying action validity may still learn internal representations that separate valid from invalid actions. c) Broader state space coverage during fine-tuning, such as from random walk data, yields more accurate recovery of the underlying world model. In summary, this work contributes a recipe for applying interpretability techniques to planning LLMs and generates insights that shed light on open questions about how knowledge is represented in LLMs.
2606.03685.paper_summary_zh: 監督式微調（SFT）改善了大型語言模型（LLMs）中的端到端傳統規劃，但這些模型是否也學會了表示和推理它們正在解決的規劃問題？由於傳統規劃問題的相對複雜性以及端到端計劃生成對LLMs所帶來的挑戰，探索這個問題一直很困難。在我們的研究中，我們設計並執行了一系列可解釋性實驗，全面檢視通過檢查微調LLMs的內部表示和生成能力來恢復世界模型。我們發現：a) 在有效行動序列上進行監督式微調使LLMs能夠線性編碼行動有效性和一些狀態謂詞。b) 雖然在使用輸出概率來分類行動有效性方面掙扎的模型，仍然可能學會將有效行動與無效行動分開的內部表示。c) 在微調期間更廣泛的狀態空間覆蓋，例如來自隨機漫步數據，能夠更準確地恢復潛在的世界模型。總之，這項工作為將可解釋性技術應用於規劃LLMs提供了一個方案，並生成了洞見，幫助解答有關知識在LLMs中如何表示的未解問題。
2606.03685.author: Patrick Emami et.al.
2606.03685.authors: Patrick Emami, Nan Qiang, Peter Graf
